summary: This week in AI news, significant developments include the EU forcing Google to share search data, xAI suing users for generating CSAM, and advancements in AI-powered game creation. Enterprise AI organizations face challenges in trust, retrieval, and deployment. Google expands AI Mode, and NVIDIA introduces new Jetson Thor computers for mainstream robotics and edge AI.
insight: The increasing use of AI in various industries, such as weather forecasting, robotics, and healthcare, highlights the need for robust security measures to prevent data sabotage and ensure user privacy and safety.
highlights: Google must give rival AI assistants and search engines greater access to key parts of Android and Google Search in Europe.
xAI files lawsuit against Grok user accused of making child sex images, raising concerns about AI-generated CSAM.
NVIDIA introduces new Jetson Thor computers to advance mainstream robotics and edge AI, enabling more efficient and compact AI supercomputers.

articles.title: It's official: EU will force Google to share search data and open up AI on Android
articles.description: Google says these changes could endanger user privacy and security.

articles.title: xAI can’t deny Grok makes CSAM anymore. So it’s suing users.
articles.description: Elon Musk's xAI files first lawsuit against Grok user accused of making child sex images.

articles.title: Fear of humanoid robots spurs human workers to strike at Hyundai auto factory
articles.description: Hyundai aims to deploy 25,000 Atlas robots starting with US factories in 2028.
